name: Statistical process control

problemSignals.description: A repeated process exhibits variation whose common or special causes are unclear.
Quality measures change over time despite nominally similar inputs.

requiredEvidence.description: Sequential measurements with timestamps, subgroup rules, and missing-data markers.
Operational measure, sampling plan, resolution, and known measurement error.

assumptions: The selected chart matches data type, subgrouping, and distributional conditions

deterministicSteps.action: Validate measure definition, ordering, subgrouping, missingness, and chart preconditions.
Calculate center line and control limits from the declared baseline and chart formula.
Apply declared special-cause rules without treating control limits as specification limits.

outputs.description: Validated ordered sample and chart-selection rationale.
Reproducible center line, limits, and plotted sequence.

whenAll: Measurement system and subgrouping are adequate
Limited baseline size or measurement uncertainty remains
Chart type or measurement system cannot be justified

contraindications: Data are one-off rather than a repeated process
Specification conformance is being inferred solely from control limits

contract: Require explicit chart and subgroup rules
Emit formulas, limits, and triggered rules
